game_info.title: Breach Protocol
game_info.concept:
Control powerful mechs from the future to defeat an alien threat. Each attempt to save the world presents a new randomly generated challenge in this turn-based strategy game. Steam URL: https://store.steampowered.com/app/590380/Into_the_Breach/ ================================================================================ Full Description: ================================================================================ Control powerful mechs from the future to defeat an alien threat. Each attempt to save the world presents a new randomly generated challenge in this turn-based strategy game.

The free major update, Into the Breach: Advanced Edition is now available!


Nearly every aspect of the game is expanded with new content, including:

  • New Mechs and Weapons: Five new mech squads and nearly forty new weapons.
  • New Challenges: More enemies, more bosses, and more mission objectives.
  • New Pilot Abilities: Four new pilots and triple the amount of pilot skills.
  • “Unfair” Mode: A more challenging difficulty to test the most seasoned tacticians.



The remnants of human civilization are threatened by gigantic creatures breeding beneath the earth. You must control powerful mechs from the future to hold off this alien threat. Each attempt to save the world presents a new randomly generated challenge in this turn-based strategy game from the makers of FTL.

Key Features:

  • Defend the Cities: Civilian buildings power your mechs. Defend them from the Vek and watch your fire!
  • Perfect Your Strategy: All enemy attacks are telegraphed in minimalistic, turn-based combat. Analyze your opponent's attack and come up with the perfect counter every turn.
  • Build the Ultimate Mech: Find powerful new weapons and unique pilots as you battle the Vek infestation across Corporate-Nation islands.
  • Another Chance: Failure is not an option. When you are defeated, send help back through time to save another timeline!
game_info.description:
**Breach Protocol** is a tactical turn-based strategy game where you command a squad of futuristic mechs to defend civilian cities from an alien bug infestation (the Vek).

On each turn, the enemy reveals their attack plans. Your goal is to command your mechs to kill the enemies or push them out of position to disrupt their attacks and save the buildings. Defend the Grid Power (civilian lives) for 5 turns to secure the region. If Grid Power reaches zero, the timeline is lost.
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Keys: Move cursor across the grid
- Space: Select Unit / Confirm Movement / Confirm Attack
- Z: Cancel Selection / Deselect Unit
- Shift: End Turn (Hold to confirm) / Reset Unit Move
- Enter: Start Game
- ESC: Pause / Unpause
- R: Restart Game (from Game Over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Verify game stability and basic state transitions under random input.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test controller will inject random valid key presses (Arrow keys, Space, Z, Shift) to simulate a chaotic player. It aims to ensure no crashes occur during phase changes and gameplay loops.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The game runs for at least 600 frames without throwing errors, and the game phase transitions correctly between PLAYING and enemy turns.

TEST_2.test_description: Test victory condition by surviving the required number of turns.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The test controller will use a defensive heuristic: positioning mechs to block enemy spawns and attacking enemies adjacent to buildings. It will aggressively end turns to advance the turn counter.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The turn counter reaches the maximum (5), triggering the GAME_OVER_WIN phase, demonstrating that the win condition is reachable.

TEST_3.test_description: Test defeat condition when Grid Power is depleted.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The controller will purposefully do nothing (skip turns continuously) while enemies attack buildings. This ensures enemies successfully damage the grid.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The Grid Power drops to 0, triggering the GAME_OVER_LOSE phase, verifying the lose condition logic.
